L1.area: GitHub Flavored Markdown extension modules and content parsing logic

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the parsing of markdown table structures. When a table marker row contains an excessively large number of columns, an unsigned 16-bit column counter wraps around, leading to heap memory corruption. This can result in information disclosure or arbitrary code execution when processing attacker-controlled markdown input.

L3.area: GFM table extension module and its row and column parsing infrastructure
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the parsing of GitHub Flavored Markdown tables where a table marker row with more than 65535 columns causes an unsigned 16-bit column counter to wrap around. This leads to heap memory corruption and missing null-safety checks on returned row structures, resulting in potential information disclosure or arbitrary code execution when processing attacker-controlled markdown content.

ground_truth.reason: The table row parsing logic uses a uint16_t column counter that wraps around when a table marker row exceeds UINT16_MAX columns, causing heap memory corruption. Additionally, callers of the row parsing function lack null checks, leading to unsafe memory access.
